caption:
Saturday was electric. Thank you, Memphis.

We talked about carrying grief and joy at the same time - and your calls carried the whole hour. This community never stops surprising me.

#morningreflections #wgsp1490 #memphis #faith #chaplaincy

Six years on the radio and I still get butterflies walking into the Beale Street studio at 5:30 AM.

This Saturday we're talking about what it means to start over - whether you're 25 or 75. Tune in WGSP 1490 AM, 7-9 AM.

#morningreflections #wgsp1490 #memphis #saturday

Started the zinnia seeds indoors this week. Every year they remind me - new life keeps its own schedule. You can't rush it.

Happy Monday, Cooper-Young.

#garden #memphis #cooperyoung #zinnias #morningpages

Brunch with my person at Cafe Eclectic - sacred Saturday tradition.

Sandra, thank you for 20+ years of the kind of friendship that tells you the truth. Couldn't do any of this without you.

#saturday #brunch #memphis #cafeeclectic #friendship

This Saturday on Morning Reflections: a conversation about what the church can learn from hospital waiting rooms - where faith meets fear with no scripts.

WGSP 1490 AM • 7-9 AM • Call in: (901) 555-1490

#morningreflections #wgsp1490 #faith #chaplain #memphis

A morning at Elmwood Cemetery. Quiet, necessary, holy ground.

We remember those we carry - and the families who waited and carried on. That kind of courage deserves more than we know how to give.

#remembrance #elmwoodcemetery #memphis #honor #remember

Christmas Eve dinner at The Country Table - pot roast, mac and cheese, cornbread, peach cobbler. Mama Dorothy in her element.

Some meals are really prayers. This was one of them.

#christmaseve #memphis #sundaydinner #thecountrytable #family

Naomi walked her first home visit as a licensed social worker this week. My daughter - who taught me what advocacy looks like up close.

Proud doesn't cover it.

#naomi #socialwork #memphisyouthservices #proudmama #memphis

Saturday's show reached into something I wasn't expecting. A caller said: 'I only came to church because the chaplain at the hospital asked me to try it one more time.' That stays with me.

Thank you for trusting us with your stories.

#morningreflections #wgsp1490 #faith #memphis #healing

Advent dawn service. Grace Community Church, Cooper-Young. I delivered the meditation as the sky turned gold over Memphis.

We say the light comes once a year. But I've watched too many people find their way back from the dark to know that's not true.

#advent #adventreflection #gracecommunitychurch #memphis #faith

Greenline this morning before the shift. Memphis was quiet and cool and exactly right.

Walking is prayer. I need you to know that.

#greenline #memphis #morningwalk #wellness #chaplain

This Saturday on Morning Reflections: Rev. Angela Dawson joins me to talk about what it means to be present - really present - when someone is in crisis. Don't miss it.

WGSP 1490 AM • 7-9 AM

#morningreflections #wgsp1490 #chaplaincy #faith #memphis

Planted lavender and rosemary along the back fence today. The bees found the lavender within the hour.

Some things don't need much convincing - they just need the right conditions.

#garden #lavender #rosemary #cooperyoung #memphis #saturday

Sixteen years at Bluff City Medical Center. I walked in on a Tuesday in October 2010 thinking I'd figure it out as I went.

I'm still figuring it out. That's probably the point.

#chaplaincy #bluffcitymedical #memphis #grateful #anniversaryreflection

Marcus home from Crescent City for fall break. We cooked Sunday dinner together - he seasoned the chicken and I stayed out of the way.

That boy is going to be fine.

#marcus #fallbreak #sundaydinner #memphis #family

Covenant Insurance, Mitchell's Flowers, and Bluff City Auto Care - thank you for keeping Morning Reflections on the air.

Your support means Memphis keeps hearing stories that matter. We are grateful.

#morningreflections #wgsp1490 #sponsors #memphis #community

Seven years on air this month. The first Saturday I sat at that mic I thought: 'who am I to do this?' Pastor Greer said: 'You're the one who showed up.'

Still showing up.

#morningreflections #wgsp1490 #anniversary #memphis #7years

CPE residents started their new rotation this week. Three of them said they were terrified. I told them: good - that means you understand the weight of it.

Being with people in the hardest moments is the most important work there is.

#chaplaincy #cpe #clinicalpastoral #memphis #bluffcitymedical
Sat with a family in the ICU at 3 AM, made the air a little lighter.
Morning Reflections live from the Beale Street studio this week.
Friday groceries and dinner at Mom's in South Memphis.
Greenline walk with worship music, two to three miles.
Sunday dinner ministry — pot roast, mac and cheese, peach cobbler.
Morning pages before dawn, coffee steam in the dark kitchen.
Booked next week's radio guest — a Memphis musician for the devotional.
Palliative-care debrief with the team, then the ethics committee.
Brenda's voice on the phone every evening, the anchor.
Garden in the fading light — zinnias, lavender, the bird feeders.
CPE hours at 28 of 50, mentoring the two residents.
Naomi checked in twice this week, my old soul.
Chamomile on the porch swing with Mercy at dusk.
Withings weigh-in, down twelve; Dexcom steady, A1C at 6.4.
Playlist curated for the show — Lauren Daigle and the Gettys.
Health-fair planning with Deacon Mosley at Grace Community.
Therapy with Dr. Whitfield — compassion fatigue and boundaries.
Marcus called about the broadcasting internship, so proud.
Saturday yoga video before the live broadcast.
Tithe to Grace Community before I budget anything else.
Cafe Eclectic brunch with Sandra, our tradition.
Sponsor check-in with Covenant Insurance and Mitchell's Flowers.
Oura readiness low today — honored it and slowed down.
Interfaith event with Rev. Dawson, the clergy network.
